rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO MARIN COUNTY** CONS. OF DONALD PAUL LERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/5/2021. 1) 12/22/2020 Order ordered as an exception to the court's local rules the second account is to cover a 4-year period from 4/21/2012 to 4/21/2016. The filed account covers a different account period than the court order 04/21/2012 to 4/22/2016. 2) The starting balance of $20,266.34 does not match the prior account ending balance of $20,266.35. 4) The $1,500.00 paid to the law firm must be explained in this account. Please file a receipt and explain the legal basis/cite legal authority in support of the payment. Please address LRSF 14.56 C. 5) Account #1 alleged at the end of the account period there was only 1 account #4934. Please explain when account #9923 was opened and if/when #4934 was closed and please provide evidence tracing all the funds from #4934 to #9923. 6) Please file a receipt and explain the request for 12/2/2019 Filing Fee: Petition, San Francisco County for $435.00. There is no such filing in the court system. 6) Please file receipts and explain entries 10/9/2020 Recorder's Fees: Court eFiling: Swift Attorney Services for $110.50 and $15.00. If no receipt is available please file legal basis/cite legal authority for the court approving this reimbursement request. 7) Matter will go on for hearing once other issues are addressed. Conservator transferred $5,000.00 from the conservatorship without court authority. Conservator used conservatorship funds for her own benefit. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/20/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/5/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 9/20/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THEODORE LUCIER JURY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The administrator retained unlawful detainer litigation counsel to recover possession of the estate real property from Nathan Jury, one of the heirs, and paid $3,355 from the estate funds to the litigation counsel without prior court approval. 2) The petition proposes deducting $3,772 from distribution to Nathan Jury for the mortgage and utility payments incurred as a result of his continuing occupancy of the estate real property from 03/31/2020 through 06/14/2020. The proposed deduction consists of the mortgage payments for April and May 2020 in the total amount of $3,028.32; mortgage payment for June 2020 in the prorated amount of $706.61; and final utility bill in the amount of $37.07. 3) The administrator's attorney requests $10,000 for his extraordinary services to the estate. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 10th Amended General Order which took effect on 06/28/2021 for more information.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Real Property Held By Trustee In Trust

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WENDY HOBSON FAMILY 2014 TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Real Property Held By Trustee In Trust
rulings.Rulings: continued to 10/5/21 at counsel's request. no later than noon on 9/24/21, a supplement to the petition must be filed pursuant to CRC 7.902 that lists all trust beneficiaries, along with their current addresses. in addition, since this petition is brought under PC 850, all heirs who are not trust beneficiaries (if any) must be identified and listed. the court is concerned about venue, which is strictly construed. the 16061.7 notice must be filed, along with a redacted copy of a trust account statement. please note, this court does not determine proper venue based on consent. a memorandum of points and authorities must be filed that addresses the issue of whether real property can be held by an individual and a trustee. (the case cited in the petition did not involve a trust). the court is interested in whether an indivual and a trustee really have unity of interests, given the difference between a human life and the life of a trust. in addition, as a trust is a relationship over property, it is not clear how there is unity of possession.
